_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: 17 Temple Demolitions in Selangor Since 2020 Despite Court Orders
context: The Malaysian Hindu Sangam documented 17 Hindu temple demolitions in Selangor between 2020 and 2025, including 4 where court injunctions were in force at the time of demolition.

cards.text: 4 temples were demolished while court injunctions were active.
cards.sub: Local authorities proceeded despite court orders. No contempt proceedings followed.

cards.h: An estimated 3,200 Hindu temples in Malaysia lack proper land title.
cards.s: Most were built on estate land before independence. When estates were developed, temples had no legal standing.
cards.d: An estimated 3,200 Hindu temples in Malaysia lack proper land title. Most were built on estate land before independence. When estates were developed, temples had no legal standing.

cards.h: The 2009 Cabinet Committee on Temple Relocation has relocated only 34 temples.
cards.s: At the current rate, the backlog of 3,200 untitled temples will take 280 years.
cards.d: The 2009 Cabinet Committee on Temple Relocation has relocated only 34 temples. At the current rate, the backlog of 3,200 untitled temples will take 280 years.

cards.h: Hindu Malaysians comprise 6.3% of the population.
cards.s: Temple demolitions disproportionately affect a minority community with limited political leverage.
cards.d: Hindu Malaysians comprise 6.3% of the population. Temple demolitions disproportionately affect a minority community with limited political leverage.

cards.text: When court orders do not stop demolitions, minority rights exist on paper but not on the ground.
Temple regularisation requires a dedicated land audit, fast-track titling process, and meaningful penalties for demolishing protected structures. The 2009 committee approach has demonstrably failed.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
